{{Quote|Sniper, sniper!|A clone trooper under attack by battle droid assassins|<ref name="BF II" />}}
The '''battle droid assassin''' was a variation of the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] used by the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation]] and the [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Confederacy of Independent Systems]] over the course of the the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Their gyroscopic stabilizers and detailed target-selection programs made them very effective [[Sniper/Legends|snipers]], which proved lethal to the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic's]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]]. They were not, however, as common as the regular B1 [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droids]], because it was costly to make a droid as accurate as an [[Species/Legends|organic]] sniper could be.

==History==
[[File:E-5s_sniper_rifle.jpg|A battle droid assassin aiming at a team of [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|ARC troopers]]|thumb|left|170px]]
During the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], [[Battle droid/Legends|battle droid]] [[Assassin/Legends|assassins]] were deployed at the [[First Battle of Geonosis/Legends|Battle of]] [[Geonosis/Legends|Geonosis]] in defense of the most important units waiting to be loaded onto [[Transport/Legends|transports]]<ref name="BF I">''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]]''</ref> and at the [[Battle of Muunilinst/Legends|Battle of]] [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]] against [[Advanced Recon Commando/Legends|Advanced Recon Commandos]].<ref name="SW:CW animation">{{CW|3}}</ref> Battle [[Droid/Legends|droid]] assassins were also used during the [[Battle of Tirahnn (Clone Wars)|Battle of Tirahnn]],<ref name="GAW">''[[Galaxy at War]]''</ref> and appeared on a number of battlefronts throughout the Clone Wars. Following the end of the conflict and the shutdown of the [[Separatist Droid Army/Legends|Separatist Droid Army]], battle droids were reactivated and used by the rogue [[Geonosian/Legends|Geonosian]] [[Gizor Dellso]] in his [[Gizor Dellso's army|army]] up until the [[Battle of Mustafar (Imperial Period)|Battle of Mustafar]].<ref name="BF II" />

==Characteristics==
[[File:Sniperdroid.jpg|A battle droid assassin|thumb|right|150px]]
Battle droid assassins were [[B-series battle droid/Legends|B-series battle droids]] produced by [[Baktoid Armor Workshop/Legends|Baktoid Armor Workshop]]. They stood 1.91 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] tall and featured [[Color/Legends|tan]] plating and black [[Sensor/Legends|sensors]] on their cranial units.<ref name="Databank" /> The [[Droid/Legends|droids]] were equipped with a Baktoid Armor [[E-5s sniper rifle/Legends|E-5s sniper rifle]] as well as a standard [[SE-14 blaster pistol|blaster pistol]], [[V-1 thermal detonator]]s, and a [[Mark IV recon droid]].<ref name="BF I guide" /> Certain models were equipped with an [[Autoturret/Legends|autoturret]]. While the units wore no special markings, some were painted with camouflage patterns to help them remain hidden from enemy forces.<ref name="BF II" /> They were [[Programmer/Legends|programmed]] with advanced targeting capabilities and to utilize their E-5s rifles to the utmost against enemy [[Soldier/Legends|soldiers]].<ref name="BF I guide" />

==Behind the scenes==
Battle droid assassins have appeared in several Clone Wars-era sources. In the [[Star Wars Battlefront (series)|''Star Wars: Battlefront'']] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] series, they serve as the Confederacy of Independent Systems sniper class.

In ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront]],'' the battle droid assassins (known as droid snipers in-game) had red markings resembling those of the [[OOM-series security droid/Legends|security battle droids]],<ref name="BF I" /> but in ''[[Star Wars: Battlefront II]]'' they were given a camo pattern.<ref name="BF II" />
